channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: BioWare Veteran Talks About Burnout On Sci-Fi RPG Exodus: ‘You Have To Be Cutting The Baby In Half All The Time’
item.content:encoded:
James Ohlen was lead designer on Baldur’s Gate 2 and worked at role-playing game powerhouse BioWare for over 22 years. He left in 2018 to cofound his own studio, Archetype Entertainment, and work on a new sci-fi RPG Exodus that people can’t help but keep comparing to Mass Effect. But he left his new studio before the game shipped under mysterious circumstances last year. He now says the experience “nearly killed me.”

“As a creative you care about everything so much, and then as the head of the studio, you have to be cutting the baby in half all the time, and having people attack your vision constantly,” he said in a new wide-ranging interview with PC Gamer. “I definitely wouldn’t put myself in that situation again; that’s not a healthy place to be.”

Exodus is an ambitious RPG shooter where time dilation effects let you see the consequences of your actions play out across different worlds over the course of centuries. It also involves Matthew McConaughey, for some reason. Wizards of the Coast is funding the project, and would seemingly love to use its deep lore and vast world-building as a springboard for new tabletop RPGs, trading card games, and potential movie or TV spin-offs. The game is set to ship in 2027, making it a project that will have spanned a pandemic, three presidential administrations, and potentially multiple console generations.

Ohlen detailed difficult contract negotiations with Peter Hamilton and challenging bureaucratic hoops at Wizards. It also sounds like heading up an entire new studio alongside being the creative lead on an entire new IP took its toll. “All the people I know that have headed up studios because they love it, and I hate it,” he said, adding that he struggled without a “hotshot executive producer” to help shield the creative side of Exodus‘ production from the higher-ups.

The game industry veteran is now working on a series of RPG adventure books with former BioWare creative director Jesse Sky instead. It doesn’t sound like he’ll be diving back into the world of video game startups anytime soon.



https://kotaku.com/bioware-veteran-talks-about-burnout-on-sci-fi-rpg-exodus-you-have-to-be-cutting-the-baby-in-half-all-the-time-2000696194
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Two New, Very Different Xbox Controllers Have Leaked Online
item.content:encoded:
Earlier today, photos of a new, unannounced Xbox controller leaked after Brazil’s Anatel regulator accidentally shared images of the yet-to-be-revealed gamepad online. And then, a few hours later, the same regulator leaked images of a different, officially not announced Xbox controller. Whoops.

On May 14, Brazilian technology news site Technoblog obtained and posted leaked images of a cloud controller designed specifically to work with Xbox game streaming. The controller is smaller than a normal Xbox gamepad, but includes all the buttons and sticks you’d expect, just arranged in a smaller, cheaper form factor. This is the kind of controller that Xbox expects people to toss in a bag and bring with them on a trip or leave on a table in a room with a TV that can stream games.

In February, The Verge reported that Xbox was developing a streaming controller that would directly connect to games through a wifi connection, similar to how Google Stadia controllers worked with the now-defunct game streaming service. This appears to be that controller. The idea being that a direct wif-fi connection has less latency than a connection via Bluetooth that has to go through a few more steps to reach the game running on a server somewhere off-site.

And then, later on that very same day, Technoblog published photos it obtained showing what appears to be the Xbox Elite 3 controller, which is likely to be a far more expensive pad compared to the leaked cloud streaming controller.

Kotaku has contacted Xbox about the leaked controllers.

The Elite 3 gamepad features an interchangeable d-pad, back paddles, and two extra buttons with yet unknown functions. It also features two new scroll wheels at the bottom of the controller that could be used to tweak controller settings, or perhaps will be a new input method. The Elite 3 will also support cloud streaming via a direct wifi connection, similar to what that other leaked Xbox controller can do. Interestingly, the Elite 3 seems to come with a removable and rechargeable battery, something that you can buy for Xbox controllers separately. (I use a Play and Charge kit and love it.)

We don’t know how much either leaked controller will cost or when they will launch, but with an upcoming Xbox event in June, it seems likely that one or both of these leaked gamepads will be announced and sold in the near future.



https://kotaku.com/two-new-very-different-xbox-controllers-have-leaked-online-2000696180
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Reveals An Ambitious Blueprint For Making It A Game More People Want To Play
item.content:encoded:
Marathon is about to wrap up its first season. It’s a brilliant game with a dwindling player base. But Bungie has some ambitious ideas for turning that around. Game director Joe Ziegler outlined what the team has learned from the past two months and how that is informing where Marathon is headed in the future. The plan includes experiments with PVE-only modes as well as more casual PVP-lite experiences. But these are just the tip of the iceberg.

“With the first season of Marathon we’ve created a strong core community,” Ziegler wrote in a new blog post on May 14. “We’re embarking on a multi-season journey built around growing from the seed of this strong community.” He said the team is looking to solve various pain points like making the extraction shooter more rewarding and less grindy, improving new player onboarding, and deepening the endgame meta.

Bungie admitted that Marathon can be “overwhelming to learn” and that its too easy for people who play alone or aren’t super skilled to hit a wall early on in their progression. The late season endgame has also been pretty stale, grenades and snipers being used to spawn rush and lobby clear in a way that saps encounters of tactical or strategic complexity. Mostly, Bungie seems to think Marathon just needs more chill.

But the development team is also planning entirely new experiences for Marathon. Ziegler hinted at “fun and mind-bending content” that includes updated maps, new runner classes, and different gear, but also “more survival experiences for different moods. This means “exploring more pure PVP, PVE, and PVP-lite experiences.” This is the first time Bungie has come out and said that Marathon might eventually expand beyond its excellent but very punishing high-stakes PVP core with more casual modes that non-PVP players can still enjoy.

PVE-only action is coming to Tau Ceti IV

Ziegler went into more detail later in the post:

In Season 2, we will be experimenting with two modes, one at the beginning of the season and one towards the middle/latter half of the season. The experimental mode at the beginning of the season will focus more on PVE, but with a light touch of PVP. The second experimental mode will be a PVE-only mode that’s focused on crews being tasked with completing objectives together and making some progress across matches.

In terms of what else is guaranteed for Marathon season 2, which goes live on June 2, Ziegler confirmed that a rotating Duos queue is returning, along with “testing some experiments” PVE and PVP-Lite modes. Players’ max vault size will also be expanding, which is music to my ears as someone constantly playing inventory Tetris in between matches. There’s also the previously revealed Night Marsh map, Cradle progression system, and new Sentinel character, all of which players will get more details on closer to the season 2 start date.

But Ziegler also gave a peak at what’s coming beyond season 2. While Bungie will be testing some of these new modes beginning in June, season 3 will bring a much deeper overhaul to the onboarding experience on Perimeter, alongside additional content. “Season 4 we’re focusing on building more depth into the existing extraction loop,” he wrote. “Season 5 we’re looking at bringing the whole ecosystem of (PV(P)VE) play together and evolving our weird sci-fi world in new ways.”

If Bungie can continue delivering rapid mid-season improvements that respond to player feedback on a weekly basis, that first year roadmap is looking pretty robust. Despite grim Steam charts and the constant specter of cuts following the game’s initial performance, Ziegler reiterated that the team is focused on shaping Marathon “beyond 2026 and into 2027.”

Update 5/14/26, 4:44 p.m. ET: Added more information and context from Bungie’s blog post. 



https://kotaku.com/marathon-reveals-an-ambitious-blueprint-for-making-it-a-game-more-people-want-to-play-2000696161
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Player Gets Banned For A Month After Taking Griefing To New Heights: ‘I Made A Mistake And Did Something F*cked Up’
item.content:encoded:
Over the weekend, a Marathon streamer who goes by NAPainter pulled off the most controversial stunt in the extraction shooter’s two-month-old history. Now Bungie is punishing him with a 30-day ban, and he’s not fighting it. “I am more than ok with this outcome,” he wrote on X. “I made a mistake and did something fucked up. I’m willing to take this.”

Footage of the “most HEINOUS, EVIL, and DISGUSTING thing” NAPainter said he’s ever done in a video game before rippled through the Marathon community earlier this week. It showed him using an out-of-bounds glitch on Cryo Archive to hide in the raid boss room until an enemy team had finished the encounter. Then he ambushed them and took all of their loot.

Cryo Clip of the Week goes to the most HEINOUS, EVIL, and DISGUSTING thing I have EVER done in any video game.

If you can get Out of Bounds and you can get into the Compiler loot room.

And you can just camp in the room for someone do compiler…..and then kill them. pic.twitter.com/OyQIgJWkYM

— NAPainter_ (@NAPainter_) May 9, 2026

Bungie later addressed the incident, saying it would patch the exploit before Cryo Archive returned this weekend, as Marathon finishes out its first season. The studio added that it would investigate what happened and clarify the rules on bannable offenses.

Part of that included kicking NAPainter from the game for 30 days, a punishment he detailed on X today after getting permission from Bungie’s community manager. “I received a 1 month suspension from Marathon a couple days ago for ‘deliberately ruining the online experience for others, either by cheating, exploiting a game glitch, or being disruptive,” he wrote. “Let me make this clear. I am more than ok with this outcome. I made a mistake and did something f*cked up. I’m willing to take this. No questions asked. No complaints given.”

With Permission from Cozmo, I am now posting about this.

I received a 1 month suspension from Marathon a couple days ago for "deliberately ruining the online experience for others, either by cheating, exploiting a game glitch, or being disruptive"

Let me make this clear. I am… pic.twitter.com/Yi36GvFvt4

— NAPainter_ (@NAPainter_) May 14, 2026

NAPainter was criticized for his stunt by other plans and admitted back when it happened that he should have revived the enemy team instead of gloating about killing them and taking their stuff. He said he’s since reached out to the players affected, all of whom received make-up reward packages from Bungie, to apologize for how he acted.

“In the mean time I am compiling a large list of every exploit I know exists or theorize to be possible within the game and handing it over to Bungie,” he continued. “It may not be much, but its my first step in making amends to Bungie. To the greater Marathon community, I apologize for how my actions affected the perception of the game as a whole. I love Marathon a lot and the fact I gave the haters more ammunition isn’t something I am proud of.”



https://kotaku.com/marathon-player-gets-banned-for-a-month-after-taking-griefing-to-new-heights-i-made-a-mistake-and-did-something-fcked-up-2000696158
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
